articles.title: OpenAI is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its IPO
articles.subtitle: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.
articles.author: Rebecca Bellan
articles.summary: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.
body: OpenAI is bringing on some big names to the team in the lead-up to its public debut: Google DeepMind AI legend Noam Shazeer and former Trump White House AI policy official Dean Ball.

Shazeer, a co-lead at Gemini and the founder of AI role-playing startup Character AI, announced his departure from Google on Wednesday . He had been at the company since 2000, leaving only for a three-year period when he left to co-found Character AI. Two years ago, Google rehired Shazeer in a $2.7 billion deal that gave the tech giant access to the startup’s technology.

The move is the latest in a series of shufflings between the top AI labs, including Google, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Meta. Shazeer is credited for being one of the foundational minds behind modern generative AI. He co-authored the seminal 2017 paper “Attention Is All You Need,” which introduced the Transformer architecture.

Before leaving Google, Shazeer had also reportedly been stirring the pot when it came to political issues. According to The Information , Shazeer voiced opinions on internal messaging boards about transgender identity and Israel’s war in Gaza that resulted in management deleting his posts.

Whether those controversies will follow him to his new employer remains to be seen. In the meantime, OpenAI is also shoring up its policy credentials by bringing Ball to the team. Ball had a brief stint last year in the White House, where he helped publish America’s AI Action Plan before stepping down to rejoin the techno-libertarian think tank the Foundation for American Innovation as a senior fellow.

“I am pleased and honored to announce that, on July 6, I’ll be joining OpenAI as leader of a new team called Strategic Futures,” Ball wrote on X on Thursday . “Our mandate will be to help the company’s leadership shape frontier AI policy.”

Ball will report directly to Chief Strategy Officer Jason Kwon. The “small, high-agency team” will focus on “matters pertaining to: catastrophic risk, recursive self-improvement, labor market impact, and the relationship between the frontier labs, governments (particularly the U.S. Federal Government), and society,” Ball wrote in a blog post .

The Strategic Futures team will cover both public-facing policy and internal governance, he added. That last is important — Ball noted that “almost by necessity,” AI labs will have to lead on AI governance decisions.
“In other words, internal governance will be more central to the future of AI than most people realize,” Ball wrote.

Ball’s decision to join OpenAI — arguably an AI favorite in the administration — comes as Anthropic battles once again with the U.S. government. Late last week, President Donald Trump ordered an export control ban on Anthropic’s latest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5 , leading to the AI firm being forced to take the models down entirely to avoid noncompliance. For anyone who had “government interference” on their S-1 risk factor bingo card, Ball is what it looks like when a company locks in its insider status while a rival is squeezed.
When you purchase through links in our articles, we may earn a small commission . This doesn’t affect our editorial independence.

Rebecca Bellan is a senior reporter at TechCrunch where she covers the business, policy, and emerging trends shaping artificial intelligence. Her work has also appeared in Forbes, Bloomberg, The Atlantic, The Daily Beast, and other publications.
keyPoints: OpenAI is bringing on some big names to the team in the lead-up to its public debut: Google DeepMind AI legend Noam Shazeer and former Trump White House AI policy official Dean Ball.

Shazeer, a co-lead at Gemini and the founder of AI role-playing startup Character AI, announced his departure from Google on Wednesday . He had been at the company since 2000, leaving only for a three-year period when he left to co-found Character AI. Two years ago, Google rehired Shazeer in a $2.7 billion deal that gave the tech giant access to the startup’s technology.

The move is the latest in a series of shufflings between the top AI labs, including Google, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Meta. Shazeer is credited for being one of the foundational minds behind modern generative AI. He co-authored the seminal 2017 paper “Attention Is All You Need,” which introduced the Transformer architecture.
paragraphTranslations: OpenAI在其公开亮相前，引入了一些重量级人物加入团队：谷歌DeepMind人工智能传奇人物诺姆·沙泽尔和前特朗普白宫人工智能政策官员迪恩·鲍尔。
沙泽尔是Gemini的联合负责人，也是人工智能角色扮演初创公司Character AI的创始人，他在周三宣布离开谷歌。自2000年以来，他一直在谷歌工作，只在离开共同创立Character AI的三年期间短暂离开。两年前，谷歌以27亿美元的交易重新雇佣了沙泽尔，让这家科技巨头得以使用该初创公司的技术。
这一举动是包括谷歌、OpenAI、Anthropic和Meta在内的顶级人工智能实验室之间一系列人员变动中的最新一次。沙泽尔被认为是现代生成性人工智能背后的基础思想之一。他共同撰写了2017年的开创性论文《注意力就是全部》，引入了Transformer架构。
据报道，在离开谷歌之前，沙泽尔在政治问题上也一直在搅动局面。据《信息报》报道，沙泽尔在内部消息板上发表了关于跨性别身份和以色列在加沙的战争的意见，导致管理层删除了他的帖子。
这些争议是否会跟随他到新雇主那里还有待观察。与此同时，OpenAI也通过引入鲍尔来加强其政策资质。鲍尔去年在白宫短暂任职，他帮助发布了美国的人工智能行动计划，然后辞职重新加入技术自由主义智库美国创新基金会，担任高级研究员。
“我很高兴也很荣幸地宣布，7月6日我将加入OpenAI，领导一个名为战略未来的新团队，”鲍尔在周四在X上写道。“我们的任务将是帮助公司领导层塑造前沿人工智能政策。”
鲍尔将直接向首席战略官杰森·权报告。这个“小而高效”的团队将专注于“涉及灾难性风险、递归自我改进、劳动力市场影响以及前沿实验室、政府（特别是美国联邦政府）和社会之间的关系的事务”，鲍尔在博客文章中写道。
他补充说，战略未来团队将涵盖面向公众的政策和内部治理。后者很重要——鲍尔指出，“几乎出于必然”，人工智能实验室将不得不在人工智能治理决策中发挥领导作用。
“换句话说，内部治理将比大多数人意识到的对人工智能的未来更为关键，”鲍尔写道。

鲍尔选择加入OpenAI——可以说是政府中的人工智能宠儿——正值Anthropic再次与美国政府较量。上周晚些时候，唐纳德·特朗普总统下令对Anthropic的最新模型Fable 5和Mythos 5实施出口控制禁令，导致这家人工智能公司被迫完全撤下这些模型以避免违规。对于任何在S-1风险因素宾果卡上有“政府干预”的人来说，当一家公司锁定其内部人士地位而竞争对手受到挤压时，鲍尔就是它看起来的样子。
当您通过我们文章中的链接购买时，我们可能会赚取少量佣金。这不会影响我们的编辑独立性。
丽贝卡·贝兰是TechCrunch的高级记者，她报道塑造人工智能的业务、政策和新兴趋势。她的作品也出现在《福布斯》、《彭博社》、《大西洋月刊》、《每日野兽》等出版物上。

paragraphs: Artificial intelligence company OpenAI is strengthening its team before its Initial Public Offering (IPO). They have hired Noam Shazeer, a former Google DeepMind employee, and Dean Ball, a former White House AI policy official. Shazeer, known for his work on generative AI, co-authored the influential 2017 paper 'Attention Is All You Need'. Ball will lead a new team at OpenAI called Strategic Futures, focusing on AI policy and governance.
paragraphTranslations: 人工智能公司OpenAI在其首次公开募股（IPO）前加强了团队。他们雇佣了前谷歌DeepMind员工Noam Shazeer和前白宫人工智能政策官员Dean Ball。以他在生成性人工智能方面的工作而知名的Shazeer，共同撰写了有影响力的2017年论文《注意力就是你需要的一切》。Ball将在OpenAI领导一个名为战略未来的新团队，专注于人工智能政策和治理。

articles.title: The White House Is Making Up Its Rules for AI in Real Time
articles.subtitle: Anthropic still can’t distribute Claude Mythos or Fable 5 after running afoul of the Trump administration. But no one can say exactly what the company did wrong.
articles.author: Maxwell Zeff
articles.summary: Anthropic still can’t distribute Claude Mythos or Fable 5 after running afoul of the Trump administration. But no one can say exactly what the company did wrong.
body: It’s been nearly a week since the Trump administration sent an export control directive to Anthropic, forcing one of the world’s leading AI labs to pull its most advanced models offline. After days of negotiations between Anthropic and the White House, the two still remain at odds about how to bring Claude Mythos and Fable 5 back. Why? Well, it depends whom you ask.

Throughout this entire debacle, Anthropic doesn’t believe it violated any concrete procedures or rules laid out by the Trump administration, according to a person close to the company. But the White House contends that Anthropic behaved recklessly, demonstrating that it can’t be trusted to safely roll out frontier technology.

This saga proved to me that we’re now officially in the Wild West era of American AI regulation. While there are few laws on the books governing frontier AI development, that doesn’t mean companies won’t end up in trouble with Trump’s White House when they cross unspoken lines.

“The problem here is that the White House has been in this extreme anti-regulatory posture, and they’re now faced with the real AI capabilities that people have been predicting for many years,” says a former White House technology official, who asked to remain anonymous to avoid jeopardizing their professional relationships. “There should have been preparation and policies to systematically deal with this, managing the benefits and risks, but instead it's just this slap-dash approach that puts the AI industry in a real quandary.”

The Trump administration has repeatedly blocked efforts to impose guardrails on the AI industry, often arguing the rules could hamper US innovation and lead the country to fall behind rivals like China. Since he returned to the White House, President Trump has signed executive orders that reversed a Biden-era effort to create a national AI framework and created a federal task force to challenge state laws that could be deemed onerous .

While WIRED and other publications have made details of the negotiations between Anthropic and the White House public over the last week, the dispute is still defined by its opaqueness. At no point has the US government clearly stated what Anthropic did wrong—the best we have is a post on X outlining the general situation from White House technology adviser David Sacks.

Ironically, the White House’s actions have likely hampered the very kind of innovation it wants to protect. The Trump administration demanded that Anthropic prohibit all foreign nationals from accessing Mythos and Fable 5, preventing many of the AI lab’s own employees from accessing its most cutting-edge models, which the company says have sped up its research and development in recent months. All of Anthropic’s customers are locked out, too, including Apple, Meta, and much of the Fortune 500.

The White House may have had legitimate reasons to be concerned about Anthropic’s models. As my colleagues and I reported on Wednesday, US officials grew concerned when they learned earlier this month that Anthropic shared Mythos with SK Telecom , a South Korean telecom giant they allege has ties to China. Separately, Amazon CEO Andy Jassy raised concerns to US Treasury secretary Scott Bessent that some of the guardrails on Claude Fable 5, a safeguarded version of Mythos, could be circumvented.

Even if these worries are legitimate, that doesn’t mean the White House handled them adequately. In the first case, Anthropic says it coordinated with the US government on the rollout of Mythos, suggesting there may have been a chance for officials to raise alarm about SK Telecom ahead of time. Anthropic has also been working with the Korean company for years, and the arrangement seemingly never caused it to run into national security problems before. And when the White House raised concerns about SK Telecom to Anthropic, it revoked access to the model immediately, we reported.

In the second case, WIRED and other outlets have written at length about how every large language model can be jailbroken to varying extents. Anthropic and independent cybersecurity researchers argue that solving jailbreaks is not an easy or isolated issue. Because AI models are probabilistic, not deterministic, tech companies can’t guarantee what exactly they will generate in response to a given prompt. If the White House won’t let Anthropic release Claude Fable 5 until the jailbreaking problem is solved, I wouldn’t hold my breath.

But ultimately, the problem here is not that the US government is trying to ensure that advanced AI models have proper safeguards or stay out of the hands of America’s foes. It’s that the Trump administration is now in a position where it has to make these regulatory decisions in real time.

I can assure you that other AI labs like OpenAI, Google, and Meta have been watching Anthropic’s misfortune with bated breath. I’ve been asking AI executives the same question all week: How can you avoid a fate similar to Anthropic’s?
keyPoints: It’s been nearly a week since the Trump administration sent an export control directive to Anthropic, forcing one of the world’s leading AI labs to pull its most advanced models offline. After days of negotiations between Anthropic and the White House, the two still remain at odds about how to bring Claude Mythos and Fable 5 back. Why? Well, it depends whom you ask.

Throughout this entire debacle, Anthropic doesn’t believe it violated any concrete procedures or rules laid out by the Trump administration, according to a person close to the company. But the White House contends that Anthropic behaved recklessly, demonstrating that it can’t be trusted to safely roll out frontier technology.

This saga proved to me that we’re now officially in the Wild West era of American AI regulation. While there are few laws on the books governing frontier AI development, that doesn’t mean companies won’t end up in trouble with Trump’s White House when they cross unspoken lines.
paragraphTranslations: 自从特朗普政府向Anthropic发出出口控制指令以来，已经将近一周了，这迫使这个世界领先的人工智能实验室之一将其最先进的模型下线。经过几天的谈判，Anthropic和白宫之间仍然对如何恢复克劳德·米索斯和法布尔5号存在分歧。为什么呢？这取决于你问的是谁。
在整个这场混乱中，根据一位接近公司的人的说法，Anthropic不认为自己违反了特朗普政府制定的任何具体程序或规则。但白宫坚称Anthropic的行为鲁莽，表明它不能被信任安全推出前沿技术。
这段经历让我确信，我们现在正式进入了美国人工智能监管的狂野西部时代。虽然很少有法律条文规范前沿人工智能开发，但这并不意味着公司在越过未言明的界限时不会遇到特朗普白宫的麻烦。
一位要求匿名以免危及其职业关系的前白宫技术官员说：“这里的问题是，白宫一直采取这种极端的反监管姿态，现在他们面临着人们多年来一直在预测的真正的人工智能能力。应该有准备和政策来系统地应对这一点，管理利益和风险，但相反，这只是这种临时拼凑的方法，让人工智能行业陷入真正的困境。”
特朗普政府一再阻止对人工智能行业施加监管的努力，经常争辩说这些规则可能会阻碍美国的创新，导致国家落后于中国等竞争对手。自从他回到白宫以来，特朗普总统签署了行政命令，撤销了拜登时代创建国家人工智能框架的努力，并创建了一个联邦特别工作组，挑战可能被视为繁琐的国家法律。
尽管WIRED和其他出版物在过去一周公开了Anthropic和白宫之间的谈判细节，但这场争议仍然以其不透明性为特征。美国政府从未明确指出Anthropic做错了什么——我们所拥有的最好的东西是白宫技术顾问大卫·萨克斯在X上发表的一篇概述一般情况的帖子。
具有讽刺意味的是，白宫的行动可能阻碍了它想要保护的那种创新。特朗普政府要求Anthropic禁止所有外国公民访问Mythos和Fable 5，阻止了人工智能实验室自己的许多员工访问其最先进的模型，该公司表示，这些模型加快了其近几个月的研究和开发。所有Anthropic的客户都被锁定在外，包括苹果、Meta和财富500强的大部分。

白宫可能有合理的理由对Anthropic的模型感到担忧。正如我和我的同事们在周三报道的那样，美国官员在本月早些时候得知Anthropic与韩国电信巨头SK Telecom分享了Mythos时变得担忧，他们声称SK Telecom与中国有联系。另外，亚马逊首席执行官安迪·贾西向美国财政部长斯科特·贝森特提出了一些关于克劳德·法布尔5号的监管措施可能被绕过的问题，克劳德·法布尔5号是Mythos的一个受保护版本。
即使这些担忧是合理的，那也不意味着白宫处理得当。在第一种情况下，Anthropic表示它与美国政府协调推出了Mythos，这表明官员们可能有机会提前对SK Telecom提出警告。Anthropic也与韩国公司合作多年，这种安排似乎从未导致它遇到国家安全问题。当白宫向Anthropic提出对SK Telecom的担忧时，它立即撤销了对模型的访问权限，我们报道了这一点。
在第二种情况下，WIRED和其他媒体已经详细讨论了每个大型语言模型都可以在不同程度上被越狱。Anthropic和独立的网络安全研究人员认为，解决越狱问题不是一个容易或孤立的问题。因为人工智能模型是概率性的，而不是确定性的，科技公司不能保证它们会对给定的提示产生什么确切的反应。如果白宫不让Anthropic发布克劳德·法布尔5号，直到越狱问题得到解决，我不会屏住呼吸。
但归根结底，问题不在于美国政府试图确保先进的人工智能模型有适当的安全措施，或者不让它们落入美国的敌人手中。而在于特朗普政府现在处于一个必须实时做出这些监管决定的位置。
我可以向你保证，其他人工智能实验室如OpenAI、谷歌和Meta一直在屏息关注Anthropic的不幸。我整个星期都在向人工智能高管提出同样的问题：你如何避免与Anthropic类似的命运？

vocabulary.example: The Trump administration made many changes.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 特朗普政府做了许多改变。

vocabulary.example: Export control is important for national security.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 出口控制对国家安全很重要。

vocabulary.example: He drove recklessly through the streets.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 他在街上鲁莽地开车。

vocabulary.example: There is a dispute between the two companies.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 这两家公司之间有争议。

vocabulary.example: Innovation is key to business success.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 创新是商业成功的关键。

vocabulary.example: National security is a top priority.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 国家安全是头等大事。

paragraphs: The Trump administration forced Anthropic, a leading AI lab, to remove its advanced AI models offline due to export control issues. Anthropic claims it followed rules, but the White House argues it acted recklessly. This situation shows the lack of clear AI regulation in the US, with the White House often blocking efforts to impose rules, fearing they might hinder US innovation. The dispute between Anthropic and the White House is unclear, with no clear statement from the US government on what Anthropic did wrong. The White House's actions may have harmed innovation, as it demanded Anthropic prohibit foreign nationals from accessing its models, affecting its own employees and customers.
paragraphTranslations: 特朗普政府因出口控制问题迫使领先的人工智能实验室Anthropic下线其先进的人工智能模型。Anthropic声称它遵循了规则，但白宫认为它行为鲁莽。这种情况显示了美国缺乏明确的人工智能法规，白宫经常阻止施加规则的努力，担心它们可能会阻碍美国的创新。Anthropic和白宫之间的争议不明确，美国政府没有明确声明Anthropic做错了什么。白宫的行动可能损害了创新，因为它要求Anthropic禁止外国公民访问其模型，影响了其自己的员工和客户。

The dispute between Anthropic and the White House is unclear, with no clear statement from the US government on what Anthropic did wrong. The White House's actions may have harmed innovation, as it demanded Anthropic prohibit foreign nationals from accessing its models, affecting its own employees and customers.
paragraphTranslations: 特朗普政府因出口控制问题迫使领先的人工智能实验室Anthropic下线其先进的人工智能模型。Anthropic声称它遵循了规则，但白宫认为它行为鲁莽。这种情况显示了美国缺乏明确的人工智能法规，白宫经常阻止施加规则的努力，担心它们可能会阻碍美国的创新。Anthropic和白宫之间的争议不明确，美国政府没有明确声明Anthropic做错了什么。白宫的行动可能损害了创新，因为它要求Anthropic禁止外国公民访问其模型，影响了其自己的员工和客户。
